mnemonics: Think of a Boolean Array as a 'Filter Fence' – it only lets the True values pass through to select data.
keyPoints: Boolean arrays are powerful tools for filtering data in NumPy and pandas (e.g., `array[boolean_array]`).
You can create Boolean arrays by applying comparison operators (>, <, ==, etc.) directly to arrays or Series.
Logical operators (& for AND, | for OR, ~ for NOT) are used to combine multiple conditions into a single Boolean array for complex filtering.
commonMistakes: Using the Python keywords 'and', 'or', and 'not' instead of the bitwise operators &, |, and ~, which will cause an error.
Forgetting to wrap individual conditions in parentheses when combining them with logical operators (e.g., `(df['A'] > 2) & (df['B'] < 5)`).
